data.body:
PASS-1 review note (criterion T3 — Integration feasibility [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS, no findings.

T3 is an overlay that applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a new, never-used capability. This plan's only integration surface is rebar's OWN already-shipped internal seams: the Inspect-AI eval seam (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`, epic e6d9) and the git-canonical prompt library (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` + `reviewers/*.md`, da27). Both are internal and already in use — T3's ANTI-FP rule explicitly classes an internal, already-used integration as not-applicable.

I verified the load-bearing facts the plan relies on rather than assuming them:
- `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` exists and ships the eval seam; the lone external dependency (Inspect AI) is consumed behind the optional `nava-rebar[eval]` extra via `guard_import("inspect_ai", ...)`, with a pinned version floor (`INSPECT_MIN_VERSION = "0.3.221"`). Auth/credential precondition for the live run is stated (the live model run "needs the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + model credentials and runs in the eval CI").
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` already contains the standing plan-review specs the plan describes — `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`, `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`, and `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` — plus `README.plan-review.md` documenting the corpus/gold-subset split. The ISF spec's three-case-type + anti-FP structure matches the plan's ACs.
- The plan's claim that prompts "live in the SHIPPED prompt library (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`)" checks out: prompts are front-matter'd `*.md` files resolved by `get_prompt` (packaged `reviewers/*.md` or `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`), so the prompt-id strings legitimately do not appear inside `prompts.py` — no integration gap.

No capability gap, no MISMATCH, no unverified external dependency to route to a SPIKE. T3 affirmations: (a) achievable-as-described — the integration is into shipped internal seams; (b) named seam/spec paths exist and MATCH; (c) auth/credential precondition for the live eval run is stated; (d) no critical capability gap requiring a SPIKE. Clean for T3.
